reasoning: All five message types required by RFC 8446 §5.1 to be aligned with a record boundary are checked in their respective processing functions. (1) ClientHello in tls_early_post_process_client_hello (statem_srvr.c lines 1903-1908): checks RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending when SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s), sending SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE on violation. (2) EndOfEarlyData in tls_process_end_of_early_data (statem_srvr.c lines 4735-4737): checks RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending unconditionally (message is TLS 1.3 only), sending SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE on violation. (3) ServerHello in tls_process_server_hello (statem_clnt.c): within the TLS 1.3 branch checks RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending and sends SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE on violation, with an explicit comment citing the key change rationale. (4) Finished in tls_process_finished (statem_lib.c lines 854-858): checks RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending when SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s), sending SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE on violation. (5) KeyUpdate in tls_process_key_update (statem_lib.c lines 706-710): checks RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending unconditionally (message is TLS 1.3 only), sending SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE on violation. All five message types have explicit record boundary checks with the correct alert type (SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE) as specified by the rule. Since RFC 8446 is a TLS 1.3 specification, checks gated on TLS 1.3 version detection are correct.

evidence.key_code_snippet: /* ClientHello */ if (SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s) && RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending(&s->rlayer)) { SSLfatal(s, SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE, SSL_R_NOT_ON_RECORD_BOUNDARY); goto err; } /* EndOfEarlyData */ if (RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending(&s->rlayer)) { SSLfatal(s, SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE, SSL_R_NOT_ON_RECORD_BOUNDARY); return MSG_PROCESS_ERROR; } /* Finished */ if (SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s) && RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending(&s->rlayer)) { SSLfatal(s, SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE, SSL_R_NOT_ON_RECORD_BOUNDARY); return MSG_PROCESS_ERROR; } /* KeyUpdate */ if (RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending(&s->rlayer)) { SSLfatal(s, SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE, SSL_R_NOT_ON_RECORD_BOUNDARY); return MSG_PROCESS_ERROR; }
evidence.expected_behavior: ClientHello, EndOfEarlyData, ServerHello, Finished, and KeyUpdate must be verified to end at a record boundary when received (no pending data remaining in the same record), and an unexpected_message alert must be sent if this check fails.
